findings.surface: 1 + 3 + 4 (container storage / loop mint collision / helper laundering — one root cause)
findings.title: Brand-name scoping, no-shadowing, and binder-resolved type equality are unstated; a mint binder that reuses a brand parameter's name launders fresh-instance endpoints into the caller's brand, through containers or through R10(b) returns
findings.program_or_defect: BRAND-1 never states which namespace brand names occupy or whether a binder may shadow one. TYPE-6's no-shadowing enumerates 'values, regions, labels'; CQ-2 and BRAND-1 insist a brand is 'a distinct sort', NOT a region — so a compliant checker may exempt brand binders from TYPE-6, and nothing else forbids the shadow. All within-body brand plumbing (form-op rows like seq_push/cq_send have no R10(a) tie) is type-name-based, so name confusion IS identity confusion. Forge: `fn evil[brand 'q, 'e](stx: &uniq 'e seq<cq_tx<'q, u64>, 4>) -> own unit writes('e), allocates(heap), traps { let ends: own cq_ends<'q, u64> = cq_new<u64, spsc, 4>(); /* mint binder spelled 'q — shadows the brand parameter; no drafted rule rejects */ match move ends { QueueEnds(tx: t, rx: r) => { seq_push(stx, move t); /* t: cq_tx<'q,u64> is name-equal to the element type: push type-checks */ } } }` Caller: mint A as `ends_a: own cq_ends<'qa,u64>`, destructure to ta/ra; `let stx: own seq<cq_tx<'qa, u64>, 4> = seq_new<cq_tx<'qa,u64>, 4>(); region 'e { evil<'qa>(stx: &uniq 'e stx); }` — evil deposits queue B's tx; `seq_pop(&uniq 'x stx)` yields it typed `cq_tx<'qa, u64>`. Now `produce<'qa>(tx: move txb, n: 1_u64)` plus `consume<'qa>(rx: move ra)` (derivation 1/3 signatures, single-carrier, declarable) run distinct physical instances behind one brand — exactly what derivation 3 asserts 'is never accepted'. Two-line return variant, no container: `fn launder[brand 'q](tx: own cq_tx<'q,u64>) -> own cq_tx<'q,u64>` shadow-mints and returns the fresh tx; R10(b) stamps it with the caller's tied identity A. This is also the concrete answer to preregistered surface 1: the candidate defense ('element type fixes a single brand binder') holds ONLY via lexical scoping + no-shadowing of brand binders, and neither is drafted — the parameter-brand seq accepts iteration-fresh endpoints the moment the mint binder reuses the parameter's name, and loop iterations then coexist in one container under one static symbol (evil with a loop body pushes every iteration's tx).
findings.minimal_fix: Add one clause to BRAND-1: brand names occupy the REGIONID lexical namespace; TYPE-6 no-shadowing/redeclaration and OWN-3-style within-function uniqueness apply verbatim to every brand binder (brand parameters, mint binders, existential-unpack binders); two brand occurrences denote the same brand iff they resolve to the same binder; and a branded binding's tracked identity is exactly env(binder) of the brand occurrence in its stated type (per-binding tags are a cache of this). With that stated, the container defense chain is provable: iteration-2's push into an iteration-1-branded seq rejects at seq_push's operand type check (distinct binders, TYPE-5 exact match), and an outer seq cannot even be declared (binder out of scope).

findings.title: Lexical-sort brand records inherit R4's place recording without R6's source freeze that makes place recording sound: a stale arena id re-attaches to a successor arena at the same root name
findings.program_or_defect: Loan tokens can never dangle from their source because R6 freezes the source place against move/rebind/drop while entries live and R7 clears entries at scope end. BRAND-1's own R4 bullet exempts ids from all of that ('endpoints/arena-ids are brand carriers, NOT loan-confined tokens'), yet keeps the place-keyed comparison. Forge, in draft vocabulary (derivation 2's ahdl/use_id): `region 'r { let a1: own arena<'r, u64> = arena_new<'r, u64>(0_u64); let id1: own ahdl<'r, u64> = arena_put(&'r a1, 7_u64); /* record: source place a1 */ let parked: own arena<'r, u64> = move a1; /* legal: id1 holds NO loan entry so R6 never engages; STOR-4 allows in-block moves */ let a1: own arena<'r, u64> = arena_new<'r, u64>(1_u64); /* re-let of the DEAD name a1 — TYPE-6 bars only live names, OWN-1 blesses reinit-by-new-let */ use_id<'r>(ar: &'r a1, id: move id1); /* R10(a) place comparison: recorded root 'a1' == resolved root 'a1' => ACCEPTED; id1 addresses the successor arena */ }` — wrong-arena addressing, the exact property the delta preamble exists to prevent (STOR-1 already names this class 'use-after-free as well-typed slot-recycling'). Second horn: read R10(a) truly 'verbatim' and use_id is dead code instead — it demands 'a live entry', and ids hold no loan entries, so every call rejects, contradicting derivation 2's claim that arenas work 'verbatim, no generalization'. Either horn is a defect; the accepting horn is a forgery.
findings.minimal_fix: State that a live lexical-sort brand record imposes an R6-shr-class freeze on its recorded source place — the place cannot be moved, rebound, assigned over, passed by own, or reached by early drop while any carrying id is live — with records expiring R7-style at the id's consumption/scope end (this is exactly the discipline that makes R4's recording sound for loans). Alternatively key places by binding instance (fresh let = fresh place identity) and say so; one of the two must be written.

findings.title: Extraction identity is undefined: branded values can enter sealed containers (SEQ-1: 'T any owned type (affine legal)') once endpoints are declassified, but no rule assigns an identity at seq_pop/cq_recv/match-out
findings.program_or_defect: BRAND-1 defines identity only at mint (cq_new), signature seeding (R10c-analog), move transfer (R8-analog), and return propagation (R10b-analog). seq_push consumes the holder binding — the per-binding tag dies; seq_pop mints a fresh binding with no drafted identity source. Three compliant implementations diverge: (a) derive from the element type's brand name — sound only under finding 1's naming fix, and IMPOSSIBLE for the lexical sort (the type says region 'r; the identity is a PLACE, and many places live in 'r — the projection is lossy, so popped arena ids cannot be re-keyed from type at all); (b) fail-closed missing-identity — silently kills every container-of-endpoints pipeline the delta's admission story cites; (c) accept unchecked — forgeable. The cross-thread variant is blessed by the drafted rules themselves: `cq_new<cq_tx<'qa, u64>, spsc, 4>()` — a queue OF endpoints (CONC-2: endpoints Sendable iff T Sendable) — moves branded values through cq_recv with the same undefined re-identification, across threads.
findings.minimal_fix: One sentence, two halves: (i) a branded value extracted from a sealed container or matched out of a compound takes identity env(binder) of the brand occurrence in its stated result/binder type (generative sort; requires finding 1's fix first); (ii) container/element positions whose type carries a LEXICAL-sort brand (arena ids) are rejected at instantiation in this delta — fail-closed, deferred to a follow-up that adds generation-keyed records.

findings.surface: 4 + 3 (declaration discipline vs the delta's own derivations)
findings.title: The tie-totality clause contradicts derivations 4 and 5: two-carrier signatures and result-only brands are rejected at declaration by the clause's own words
findings.program_or_defect: Clause: "'q must TIE to exactly one carrying argument ... zero or several carriers, or a 'q named only by a result, reject the declaration." Derivation 4's forgery-negative `fn f['q](tx: cq_tx<'q, u64>, rx: cq_rx<'q, u64>)` has TWO carriers of 'q — under the clause f is undeclarable, so the celebrated R10(a) call-site rejection can never fire; the draft's central negative example is unreachable in its own system. Derivation 5's `fn make() -> own cq_ends<'q, u64>` names 'q ONLY in the result — the same clause rejects the declaration, so mint-and-return is forbidden outright, not merely 'gated on the BRAND-MINT binder spelling' as the draft claims. Note the danger in the obvious sloppy repair: 'tie to the first carrier only' without saying R10(a) checks ALL carriers reopens derivation 4's forgery (rx unchecked => txA+rxB accepted).
findings.minimal_fix: Rewrite the tie clause: a brand parameter requires AT LEAST one carrying parameter; one designated carrier seeds the body identity, and R10(a) verifies EVERY carrying argument's identity against that one identity (mirroring how R10(a) already ranges over 'each confined argument'); a brand carried only by the result is legal solely under the existential-pack rule and is spelled as such (fold the BRAND-MINT carve-out into the clause, not just the binder spelling).

findings.surface: own attack: ep-discipline transport (beyond the preregistered five)
findings.title: Endpoint types drop the ep discipline (cq_tx<'q, T> has no spsc/mpmc tag), so a brand-generic body cannot evaluate CQ-3's spsc-clone rejection — the permissive resolution mints a second live producer on an spsc queue
findings.program_or_defect: Pre-BRAND-1 this was harmless: the RED BOX confined endpoints to the mint scope, so the checker always saw the local cq_new and knew ep. BRAND-1 removes the confinement without transporting the discipline. `fn fan[brand 'q, 'x](tx: &'x cq_tx<'q, u64>) -> own cq_tx<'q, u64> reads('x), writes('q) { let t2: own cq_tx<'q, u64> = cq_tx_clone(tx); return move t2; }` — the row says '(mpmc only; spsc is a compile-time reject citing CQ-3)', but inside fan the brand is generic: ep appears in neither the endpoint type nor the brand-parameter spelling, and brands are not monomorphization keys, so there is no instantiation point at which the reject can evaluate. Caller: mint spsc A, `fan<'qa>(tx: &'x ta)` — nothing at the call site checks ep. If the checker accepts, two live producers exist on one spsc queue — the direct single-producer/D1 break the delta preamble names. OWN-8 reject-when-unsure arguably saves soundness by rejecting every clone in brand-generic bodies, but then all mpmc fan-out helpers (named consumers of this delta) are unwritable and the row's 'compile-time reject citing CQ-3' is undischargeable either way — the outcome is undetermined by the drafted text with one catastrophic branch.
findings.minimal_fix: Make the brand parameter carry its instance signature: spell `brand 'q : conc_queue<T, ep, K>` (or minimally `brand 'q : ep`) so every brand-generic body knows the discipline; cq_tx_clone/cq_rx_clone check ep against it, and a clone call under a discipline-free brand parameter rejects citing CQ-3 (fail-closed stated, not inferred from OWN-8).

findings.title: BRAND-1 declassifies endpoints from ratified R1's opaque-confined list by assertion, with no amendment text — the entire container/send surface it opens has no replacement rules
findings.program_or_defect: Ratified R1: 'Sealed-form token, cursor, guard, and endpoint result types are opaque confined types declared in the form table.' Under R1+R2, seq<cq_tx<...>> is ill-formed and endpoints can never be 'stored or sent' — surfaces 1 and 3(c) are closed today. BRAND-1's R4 bullet flatly asserts the opposite ('endpoints/arena-ids are brand carriers, NOT loan-confined tokens, so R2 stack-confinement does not bind them') while Delta-1 open Q1 admits the boundary predicate 'is unresolved and is the first thing hostile review must pin'. The draft thus silently amends a ratified rule, opens affine-element container storage (SEQ-1 admits it) and Sendable transfer, and drafts zero rules for what it opened — findings 1 and 3 exist exactly in that opening.
findings.minimal_fix: Either (a) ship an explicit amendment (AMD-6) in the same change: strike 'endpoint' (and the new id types) from R1's opaque-confined list, define the brand-carrier class with its storage rules (findings 1/3 fixes) and the Sendable predicate; or (b) the smaller delta: keep endpoints R1-confined in BRAND-1 v1 — the cross-fn tie machinery still lands (R2 permits own-mode call arguments), containers stay closed by R1/R2, mint-and-return stays BRAND-MINT-gated, and spawn-capture moves to Delta 1's review where it already sits.

findings.title: Derivation 1's signatures use brand-in-effect-row (reads('q), writes('q)) which EFF-1 does not admit and OPEN-FLAG (BRAND-EFFECTS) leaves undrafted — the delta's flagship derivation is unwritable as drafted
findings.program_or_defect: EFF-1's closed grammar: effect := reads(REGIONID+) | writes(REGIONID+) | allocates(...) | traps, over REGIONS; CQ-2 says a brand is not a region, and S.3's own OPEN-FLAG (BRAND-EFFECTS) says 'brand-in-row spelling is kernel-delta work'. BRAND-1's derivation 1 declares `fn produce['q](tx: own cq_tx<'q, u64>, n: own u64) -> own unit reads('q), writes('q)` — an ill-formed row under every drafted rule. Worse, EFF-2's two-way exhibits check makes ANY helper calling cq_send (row: reads('q), writes('q)) undeclarable: the body exhibits effects the row grammar cannot spell. So 'adoptable independently of concurrency; same-function-boundary endpoint helpers land immediately' (adoption note) is false on the drafted text — nothing lands until brand effects are spellable.
findings.minimal_fix: Include the BRAND-EFFECTS clause in BRAND-1 itself: EFF-1 gains brand-sorted effect atoms (reads/writes over brand names) with EFF-2 exhibits and OWN-12 instantiation rules stated (brand effects instantiate through ties, never meet OWN-5 places); or rule that queue-op effects are classed under heap/receiver regions and strike 'q from all rows and exemplars, in the same change.

findings.title: Delta 6's companion text classes brand parameters 'monomorphization-time'; a per-execution fresh skolem cannot key monomorphization — loop mints would demand unbounded instantiations
findings.program_or_defect: TAG-1: 'the natural companion to BRAND-1's brand parameter — both are non-type/non-region/non-const monomorphization-time parameters.' True for tags (they select sealed monomorphizations), category error for brands: `loop @l { let ends: own cq_ends<'q, u64> = cq_new<u64, spsc, 4>(); ... }` mints a fresh identity per execution; an implementation that keys instantiation on brand identity cannot enumerate them (monomorphization diverges or falls back to one shared instance — the exact hoisting open Q2 fears). BRAND-1's own open Q4 wants brands 'provably compile-time-only'; the two deltas pull an implementer in opposite directions.
findings.minimal_fix: One sentence in both deltas: brand parameters are check-time-only, erased before monomorphization, and never instantiation keys (unlike tags); FN-2/FN-6 treat brand arguments like region arguments. Then record loop freshness (open Q2) as a theorem of finding 1's scoping plus R12 back-edge equality — the mint binder is body-scoped, so no cross-iteration coexistence is expressible — instead of leaving it an open question in an authority-carrying delta.

findings.title: Sort exclusion is stated in one direction only: region-in-brand-position, brand-in-borrow-position, brand args at call-site targs, and assumed-distinctness of brand parameters are all unstated
findings.program_or_defect: CQ-2 rejects a brand instantiating a REGION parameter; nothing rejects the converse. `fn g['r](tx: own cq_tx<'r, u64>)` — a lexical region in the brand slot — is well-formed under drafted text (uninhabitable today only because cq_new is the sole mint and CQ-2 blocks the call-site instantiation; a defense by accident, not by rule). Likewise `&'q p` with a brand name, and the call spelling `produce<'qa>(...)` (a brand lexeme in a GRAM-3 targ position that grammatically admits only type|REGIONID|const) rely on unstated sort checks; and nothing states that two distinct brand parameters are assumed-distinct (the OWN-3 'distinct caller-supplied regions are incomparable' analog) — the tag-equality machinery happens to reject, but the posture should be written.
findings.minimal_fix: One clause: every apostrophe-lexeme occurrence resolves with a sort; a brand-sorted position (form brand slots, brand targs) accepts only brand-sorted names and a region-sorted position (borrow_expr, region_params, effect atoms, region targs) only regions, each direction a compile-time reject citing BRAND-1/CQ-2; distinct brand parameters are assumed-distinct and an R10(a) equality between them fails closed.

findings.title: Tag/brand smuggling and the R6 sole-loan exception are defended by existing clauses; S.3's RED BOX must be rewritten at adoption
findings.program_or_defect: Verified held: (a) a tag cannot sit where a brand is required nor vice versa — FORM-3 lexical classes separate them (TAG is a lowercase IDENT, brand is apostrophe-prefixed) and TAG-1 resolves tags positionally against the form's closed set, so `cq_new<u64, 'qa, 4>()` and `cq_tx<spsc, u64>` both reject at the sort/parse level; the lowercase collision between TAG and const-IDENT (GRAM-3 const := IDENT) is resolved by declared parameter sorts, positionally and context-free — keep that sentence. (b) The R6 sole-holder exception never touches brands: endpoints/ids are never loan holders, so the exception can never license an extra mint for them, and same-statement move+borrow of an endpoint is rejected by AMD-1 pseudo-entries/OWN-5. Residual doc defect: S.3's RED BOX ('Threading 'q through any fn signature is rejected'; ''q never appears in a parameter type') flatly contradicts BRAND-1's signatures.
findings.minimal_fix: At adoption, rewrite S.3's RED BOX and OPEN-FLAG (BRAND-CROSS-FN) in the same change that lands BRAND-1, and extend AMD-4's R12 comparison domain to include brand records of outside-declared bindings (inert today, guards future rot).

lens: FORGERY — construct programs where endpoints/ids of distinct instances are confused (accepted where they must reject); effort concentrated on preregistered surfaces 1 (container storage) and 3 (two mints colliding on one identity), then the remaining surfaces and two attacks of my own (ep-discipline transport, brand effect rows).
verdict: REJECT AS DRAFTED — BRAND-1 does not survive. Two FATAL forgeries stand: (1) brand-binder shadowing launders fresh-instance endpoints into a caller's brand (through containers or R10(b) returns) because brand-name scoping/no-shadowing/binder-resolved equality are unstated and the draft's own sort-distinctness argument exempts brands from TYPE-6; (2) lexical-sort brand records keep R4's place keying while shedding R6's source freeze, so a stale arena id re-attaches to a successor arena across a move + same-name re-let — wrong-arena addressing, the UAF class the delta exists to prevent. Around them: the tie-totality clause rejects the draft's own derivations 4 and 5 at declaration; extraction-from-container identity is undefined (and unrecoverable-from-type for the lexical sort); endpoints are declassified from ratified R1 by assertion with no amendment; ep discipline does not transport through brand parameters (spsc clone in brand-generic helpers is undetermined with a two-producers branch); and the flagship derivation's reads('q)/writes('q) rows are unspellable under EFF-1. The core architecture is sound — R10(a) as tag equality, generative-vs-lexical sort separation, seeding/transfer reuse, and the TAG-1 lexical split all repelled direct attack; every successful forgery exploits an UNSTATED rule beside the core, and each has a one-clause fix. Required before re-review: the brand-name scoping clause, the lexical-sort source freeze (or instance-keyed places), the extraction-identity rule with lexical-sort storage fail-closed, the tie-clause rewrite (all carriers checked against one identity; existential pack carved out), the R1 amendment or endpoints-stay-confined retreat, ep-carrying brand parameters, and the BRAND-EFFECTS clause. Delta 1 must not proceed on this text.
hardest_failed: Seed-unification laundering across two brand parameters: fn relay['q1,'q2](tx: cq_tx<'q1,u64>, rx: cq_rx<'q2,u64>) internally calling a same-brand pair['q](tx, rx) to merge a caller's A/B endpoints under one brand. DEFEATED: R10(c)-analog seeding gives 'q1 and 'q2 distinct opaque tags, and R10(a) is a pure equality test on tags with no unification machinery anywhere in the delta ('no unification search, no fixpoint ... the identity comparison is an equality test on tags'), so the inner call rejects for every caller and no drafted operation can equate two live brand identities.

Cross-sort smuggling of a lexical region into a brand position: declare fn g['r](tx: own cq_tx<'r, u64>) with g's REGION parameter in the brand slot, then feed it a real endpoint to strip its generative identity. DEFEATED: the call must instantiate region parameter 'r with the argument's brand, which CQ-2 rejects verbatim ('a brand ... may never instantiate a region parameter; a row generic over a region rejects a brand argument'), and cq_new is the only endpoint mint, so no value of a region-branded endpoint type is constructible — the type is uninhabited and the signature can never meet a value (the converse sort rule should still be stated, per the MINOR finding).

Re-branding by transfer: let t2: own cq_tx<'q2, u64> = move t1 (and rebind variants) to relabel a live queue-A endpoint as queue-B's brand, exploiting R8's holder renaming. DEFEATED: TYPE-5 exact stated-type matching at every let makes the brand occurrence part of the compared type, R8-analog text pins 'kind/identity never changes in transfer', rebind demands an identical type, and no cast row exists for branded types (OP-8/LOAD-2 keep reinterpret scalar-only) — so a move can rename the holder but never the brand.

findings.surface: Preregistered 2 (two-sorts boundary) / lexical-brand half of the generalized R10(a)
findings.title: Arena-id place-identity check has no firing condition: same-region two-arena forgery is accepted as drafted
findings.program_or_defect: region 'r { let a1: own arena<'r,u64> = ...; let a2: own arena<'r,u64> = ...; let id2: own ahdl<'r,u64> = /* minted from a2 */; let x: &'r u64 = use_id['r](ar: &'r a1, id: id2); }  — All TYPES match (both ids are ahdl<'r,u64>; two arena values legally share one region 'r). The draft says arenas are 'R9 tie + R10(a) PLACE comparison verbatim, no generalization', but verbatim R10(a) opens 'each CONFINED argument must hold ... a live entry' — and the draft itself rules ahdl a brand carrier, NOT a loan-confined token (R4 interaction: 'a value is a loan holder OR a brand carrier'), so id2 holds no entry and verbatim R10(a) is silent; the generalized check fires 'per brand parameter', but derivation 2's 'a is a plain region parameter ('Arenas need only the brand-parameter-is-a-region-parameter reading'), so it never fires either. No drafted clause compares id2's minting arena (a2) with the tied argument (a1): id2 addresses a1, breaking the delta's own claim 'the id addresses only the tied arena'. Root cause: 'its identity IS 'r's source place' is ill-defined — a REGION has no source place (many arenas per region); only R4 holders record source places, and ahdl is not a holder.
findings.minimal_fix: Give the lexical half an explicit trigger and record: (1) a place-sort brand carrier's identity record is created at its minting form op (e.g. arena_put['r](ar: &uniq 'r arena<'r,T>, v) -> own ahdl — result identity := the RESOLVED PLACE of the receiver argument, not the region); (2) the generalized R10(a) fires for EVERY argument whose type carries a brand-sorted parameter, confined or not, place-sort or generative — 'a missing record or any mismatch rejects'; (3) delete the 'verbatim, no generalization' claim for arenas — the carrier is not confined, so verbatim reuse is false.

findings.surface: Mechanism fidelity: R9-analog tie rule vs the five must-pass derivations
findings.title: No consistent reading of the drafted tie rule passes derivations 1, 2, and 4 — the rule wrong-rejects the delta's own purpose cases at declaration
findings.program_or_defect: Drafted rule: "'q must TIE to exactly one carrying argument ... (the R9 totality discipline verbatim: zero or several carriers ... reject the declaration)". Reading A (carrier reading, the drafted sentence): fn use_id['a](ar: &'a arena<'a,T>, id: ahdl<'a,T>) has TWO carriers of 'a (ar and id) -> declaration REJECTS (derivation 2 dead); fn f['q](tx: cq_tx<'q,u64>, rx: cq_rx<'q,u64>) has TWO carriers -> declaration REJECTS, contradicting derivation 4's own text ('the single brand parameter 'q requires BOTH arguments tied to it') and wrong-rejecting every legitimate both-ends-of-one-queue helper. Reading B (R9-verbatim loan reading: tie to a borrow-mode parameter; self-tie needed for an own-mode parameter rejects): fn produce['q](tx: own cq_tx<'q,u64>, n: own u64) has ZERO borrow-mode carriers of 'q -> declaration REJECTS — the factored producer loop, the round-3 killer the delta exists to admit. Additionally, under Reading A only the single tie binding is seeded (R10c-analog: 'seeded with each brand parameter' names no binding-level assignment), so derivation 2's id enters the body with NO recorded identity and every downstream branded use rejects on missing-record.
findings.minimal_fix: Replace exactly-one-tie for brands with an all-carriers rule: every parameter whose type names brand 'q is a carrier; a declaration needs at least one PARAMETER carrier (result-only 'q rejects, absent the existential-pack return); at a call, ALL carrier arguments' recorded identities must be pairwise equal and equal to the instantiation of 'q; at entry, EVERY carrier parameter's binding is seeded with the rigid parameter symbol.

findings.title: Container transit loses the identity: push is unchecked, pop has no identity source; the gates-record element-type defense is not derivable from the drafted rules
findings.program_or_defect: let e1: own cq_ends<'b1,u64> = cq_new<u64,spsc,3>(); let e2: own cq_ends<'b2,u64> = cq_new<u64,spsc,3>(); /* destructure to t1/r1, t2/r2 */ let s: own seq<cq_tx<'b1,u64>,2> = seq_new(); region 'e { seq_push['e](&uniq 'e s, move t1); seq_push['e](&uniq 'e s, move t2); } let p: own Option<cq_tx<'b1,u64>> = seq_pop['e](&uniq 'e s); — Where does t2's push reject? NOWHERE in the drafted text: seq_push declares no brand parameter, so the generalized R10(a) is silent; t2's per-BINDING record (the draft's stated tracking design) is silently discarded with the consumed binding; and a brand-vs-element-type comparison cannot even be spelled (GRAM-3 targ has no brand production, so seq<cq_tx<'b1,u64>,2> is ungrammatical). On pop, the fresh binding has NO drafted identity source (not R8 — no move statement; not R10(b) — no brand parameter on the row), so under the strict missing-record-rejects reading ALL popped endpoints are unusable (silent wrong-reject of every endpoint-pool pattern, unstated anywhere), and under the natural repair (seed the popped identity from the element type) the mixed push above LAUNDERS: t2 pops as 'b1 and pairs with r1 in any f['q] — cross-instance forgery. The candidate defense (element type fixes a single binder; loop-iteration brands cannot enter one container because the binder is loop-local and outer types cannot name it) is CORRECT but rests entirely on brand instantiations being nominal type constituents compared by TYPE-5 at every argument position — a rule the draft never states and the grammar cannot express.
findings.minimal_fix: Make brand instantiations nominal type constituents: add the brand targ production (GRAM-3) and state that TYPE-5 exact-match compares brand positions at every argument, return, construction, and element-type site; add the invariant 'a branded binding's recorded identity always equals its type's brand instantiation' (established at let, preserved by move/push, re-derived at pop from the element type — sound because every push was exact-matched). Then the push of t2 rejects at seq_push argument matching (cq_tx<'b2,u64> != cq_tx<'b1,u64>) and pop is sound.

findings.surface: Mechanism fidelity: must-pass derivation walk (wrong-reject audit) + preregistered 5 spelling gaps
findings.title: Zero of the five derivations check under the drafted text against the current kernel/optables — four load-bearing dependencies are undrafted and the adoption note misstates what BRAND-MINT gates
findings.program_or_defect: Derivation 1's signature 'fn produce['q](tx: own cq_tx<'q,u64>, n: own u64) -> own unit reads('q), writes('q)' fails on: (a) EFF-1's closed row grammar admits REGIONID-only effects and OWN-12 instantiates callee rows 'at the actual regions' and checks them against caller borrows under OWN-5 — undefined over brands; S.3's OPEN-FLAG (BRAND-EFFECTS) says brand-in-row spelling is kernel-delta work and BRAND-1, which IS that kernel delta, never drafts it; (b) the caller call 'produce<B>(tx: move t, ...)' needs a caller-side NAME for the minted brand and a brand targ — GRAM-3 targ := type | REGIONID | const has no brand production (TAG-1 amends GRAM-2/GRAM-3 for tags; BRAND-1 amends neither), and naming B is exactly OPEN-FLAG (BRAND-MINT), so BRAND-MINT gates derivations 1 and 3 (every first call from the mint scope), not only derivation 5 — the adoption-ordering claim 'endpoint helpers land immediately; only the mint-and-return path waits on BRAND-MINT' is false; (c) the identity hop cq_new -> ends -> (match destructure per CQ-4) -> tx binding is unwritten: the draft wires R8 (move statements) and R10 (calls) but the OWN-13 match-payload binding of a branded field is in neither list (R11 covers confined enums only; endpoints are not confined), and CQ-4's sealed-enum match is itself OPEN-FLAG (SEALED-MATCH); under missing-record-rejects, derivation 1 dies at its own destructure. Derivation 2 additionally references ahdl, which exists in no table (see MINOR finding).
findings.minimal_fix: Draft the four clauses inside BRAND-1 or mark the delta explicitly BLOCKED on them: a brand-effects rule (or strip reads('q)/writes('q) from the derivation and defer with a flag), the GRAM-2 'brand' gparam and GRAM-3 brand-targ productions, the BRAND-MINT dependency restated as gating derivations 1/3/5, and one sentence: 'a match destructure of a brand-parameterized sealed enum binds each branded payload with the scrutinee's recorded identity.'

findings.title: No callee-side return-identity check exists: the loan scaffolding's return equality is vacuous for brand carriers, and the caller stamps the result unconditionally
findings.program_or_defect: fn launder[brand 'p, brand 'r](a: own cq_tx<'p,u64>, b: own cq_tx<'r,u64>) -> own cq_tx<'p,u64> ... { return move b; } — The drafted (b) clause is CALLER-side only ('a branded result propagates its brand to the tied argument's identity'), stamping the result with the 'p instantiation regardless of what the body returned. The claimed reuse of the loan return check (R10(c): 'the live entries on tied parameters must equal exactly the signature-implied caller effect') verifies NOTHING here: brand carriers hold no loan entries, so the entry-based equality is vacuously true. Rejection of launder exists only if brand-nominal type equality at return (cq_tx<'r,u64> != declared cq_tx<'p,u64>) is normative — which is exactly the unstated rule of the container finding; an implementer following the draft's stated design ('the checker tracks per branded BINDING a brand identity', checks at brand-tied calls) may legitimately erase brands from type comparison, and then launder ACCEPTS and its output pairs a queue-R tx with queue-P's rx in any f['q]. Same class of hole for a mint-and-return body returning a wrong-brand end once BRAND-MINT lands.
findings.minimal_fix: One sentence in the (b) clause: 'at every return of a branded result, the returned value's recorded identity must equal the declared result brand's identity (the seeded rigid symbol, or the fresh existential for a pack); mismatch rejects' — plus the brand-nominal type-equality rule from the container fix, which makes the two checks mutually redundant by design rather than both absent.

findings.title: The region/brand sort wall is stated in one direction only, and the arena reading gives one apostrophe-name two sorts
findings.program_or_defect: Brands and regions share the apostrophe lexical class (FORM-3 REGIONID). CQ-2 states only 'a brand can never instantiate a region parameter'; the converse — a region (or any place-sort identity) cannot instantiate a 'brand'-declared parameter — is nowhere, and the declaration-site sort environment (every apostrophe-name is region-sorted or brand-sorted at its binder, checked positionally at every targ/bracket use) is unwritten. No forgery constructs (identity origins are form-controlled: generative symbols only at cq_new, places only at loan issue/arena mint, and the identity domain is a discriminated sum, so place==skolem is unsatisfiable — confusion attacks only wrong-reject), but fail-closed demands the wall be text, and derivation 2's 'a is simultaneously a lexical region (borrow liveness, OWN-4) and a brand (identity), i.e. one name carrying two sorts with no stated resolution — FORM-1 one-spelling friction and an ambiguity the sort environment must settle. TAG-1 cross-smuggling, by contrast, is defended once the brand targ production exists: tags are lowercase IDENTs, brands apostrophe-spelled — lexically disjoint classes (FORM-3), positional sort from the form's gparam list (META-2).
findings.minimal_fix: Add: 'each apostrophe-spelled name carries a declared sort (region or brand) fixed at its binder; a targ/bracket position's expected sort comes from the callee's parameter list; any cross-sort instantiation rejects, both directions.' State how a region-parameter-doubling-as-brand (arena) is sorted, or eliminate the dual reading by requiring the brand keyword there too.

findings.surface: Mechanism fidelity: the lexical half has no witness form
findings.title: ahdl<'a,T> exists nowhere — the place-identity half of the generalized comparison is drafted against a phantom, and its authority payload (which check the brand elides) is never named
findings.program_or_defect: grep over the kernel spec and all optables finds ahdl only in BRAND-1's derivation 2 itself. The kernel arena (TYPE-2, STOR-4) is a box-like owned value with no handle/id form, no mint op, no lookup op; so there is no row on which to hang the identity record's creation, no copy/move semantics for handles (R8 covers moves; if handles are copy — the natural design — record-duplication-on-copy is needed and unstated), and no named runtime check that the brand tie licenses eliding (the actual authority payload of 'the id addresses only the tied arena'). The FATAL finding's fix cannot even be verified until these rows exist.
findings.minimal_fix: Either draft the minimal arena-handle rows inside BRAND-1 (mint op creating the place record, lookup op consuming it, copy semantics, and the explicit statement of which validity check the brand does/does not elide), or cut the lexical-region half from BRAND-1 entirely and land the delta as generative-brands-only (queues), leaving ratified R10(a) untouched for places.

findings.title: Loop-minted freshness and the mint binder are the I3 defense yet remain open questions, contradicting 'adoptable independently ... land immediately'
findings.program_or_defect: Open questions 1-2 ARE the freshness mechanism: one syntactic cq_new site = one static symbol, so cross-iteration soundness rests entirely on lexical binder scoping (a loop-local brand name cannot appear in any outer binding's, container's, or return's spelled type, so two iterations' endpoints can never coexist under one static symbol) — an argument that itself requires the brands-in-types rule and TYPE-6 scoping applied to brand names, none of it stated. The existential unpack (derivation 5) additionally must FORCE a fresh binder: if the BRAND-MINT spelling ever lets a caller write let a: own cq_ends<'b_existing,u64> = make(); the unpack asserts instance equality with an existing brand and a's tx pairs with the existing rx — the two-calls-confused forgery. An authority-carrying delta whose I3 surface is 'open question' is not adoptable as the ordering section claims.
findings.minimal_fix: Promote to rules before adoption: (1) a brand binder is a lexically scoped name (TYPE-6 discipline; loop-body binders die per iteration — the OWN-11-analog falls out of scoping, no hoisting rule needed); (2) the existential unpack binder is always fresh — naming an existing brand there is a hard reject; (3) record that per-site static symbols are sound because coexistence is unspellable, as the normative argument.

findings.title: The one-pass O(statements x env) claim survives only under the type-constituent redraft, not under the drafted per-binding-record design
findings.program_or_defect: As drafted ('a brand identity is a per-binding tag in the same decidability environment'), fixing the container hole inside the record design would require per-ELEMENT identity records inside seq/table values — no longer a per-binding env, no longer the M1 decidability posture. Under the type-constituent redraft (brands compared as nominal type arguments plus per-binding records kept consistent with types), every check is a positional lookup and tag equality and the cost claim holds, including the multi-carrier tie fix (k comparisons per call, k bounded by the signature).
findings.minimal_fix: State the cost claim conditionally on the type-constituent design; this is an additional reason to redraft on that basis rather than repairing the record design.

lens: MECHANISM FIDELITY — whether BRAND-1's claimed R4/R8/R9/R10 reuse actually carries brand identities: tie/seeding audit, return propagation, transfer edge cases, wrong-reject walk of the five must-pass derivations, and the one-pass cost claim; plus the five preregistered surfaces (container storage, two-sorts boundary, mint collisions, helper-chain laundering, TAG-1/R6 interaction).
verdict: DOES NOT SURVIVE — REDRAFT REQUIRED. One FATAL: the lexical-place half's identity check has no firing condition (the carrier is not confined, so verbatim R10(a) is silent; the parameter is not brand-sorted, so the generalized check is silent), and the same-region two-arena program forges the delta's central authority claim. Four MAJORs: the tie rule wrong-rejects derivations 1, 2, and 4 at declaration under every consistent reading (a wrong-reject on the factored producer loop defeats the delta's purpose); container transit loses the identity and the gates-record element-type defense is not derivable from the drafted text (strict reading dead-ends all popped endpoints; the natural pop repair launders instances); zero of five derivations are spellable against the current kernel (brand targs, brand effect rows, mint-scope brand names, and destructure identity flow all undrafted — BRAND-MINT gates derivations 1 and 3, not just 5, so the 'lands immediately' adoption claim is false); and the callee-side return-identity check is missing (loan-entry return equality is vacuous for entry-less brand carriers — the launder function). The queue-side intent is salvageable and no runtime forgery was found against the generative half under the strictest fail-closed reading: redraft with brand instantiations as nominal type constituents compared by TYPE-5 everywhere, an all-carriers tie rule, an explicit mint/destructure/push-pop/return identity chain, the two-direction sort wall, and freshness promoted from open questions to rules — then re-run hostile review.
hardest_failed: Recursion mint-collision (preregistered 3): fn rec[brand 'q](prev: own cq_tx<'q,u64>) mints a local queue ('b) via cq_new and recurses rec<'b>(prev: move tx_b), trying to make activation-2 pair activation-1's endpoint with its own same-site mint (both are one static symbol). FAILED: the R10(c)-analog seeds the body ONCE from the signature with a rigid 'q and the caller's instantiation is invisible inside the callee ('the caller trusts only the signature', R10), so prev ('q-rigid) never compares equal to the local mint symbol and cross-activation pairing is statically unspellable — sound even though both identities are dynamically same-site mints.

Endpoint duplication for a real SPSC two-producer race (preregistered 5, R6 angle): tried R8 transfer edge cases, R11-style renames, and R6's sole-holder consume-and-reissue statement to get two live tx values of one runtime queue. FAILED: R8 'no operation duplicates a holder or an entry' plus OWN-1 affinity give each runtime endpoint at most one live owner; brand carriers hold no loan entries, so R6's sole exception (keyed to holder h's entries on P) can never engage or launder them; and spsc clone rows are compile-time rejects citing CQ-3, selected by TAG-1's closed ep tagset with no smuggling path (tags are lowercase IDENTs, brands apostrophe-spelled — disjoint lexical classes per FORM-3). Brand mislabeling alone therefore never manufactures a second producer; the brand's real payload is instance-coupling (pair-protocol and arena-addressing authority), which is where the FATAL lives.

Cross-sort identity smuggling (preregistered 2): constructed calls passing a place-identified value where a generative symbol is required and vice versa (e.g. a fn typing cq_tx<'r,...> at a region parameter, or feeding an arena-tied value into a brand 'q slot) to satisfy the generalized R10(a) with the wrong sort. FAILED: identity origins are form-controlled — generative symbols arise only at cq_new (CQ-2/CQ-4), place identities only at loan issue (R5) or the arena tie — and the identity domain is a discriminated sum, so place == skolem is unsatisfiable and every confusion program only wrong-rejects; no accepting instance found (the two-direction sort wall should still be written — filed as MINOR).
